fullName: TMO4+ Summary Decision Document — Connections Reform
authority: Ofgem decision approving CUSC modifications CMP434 and CMP435 (plus Grid Code GC0166)
file: 05 - Connections Reform (TMO4+)/TMO4+ Summary Decision Document - April 2025.pdf
summary: Ofgem's formal decision approving the most significant overhaul of GB transmission connections in decades. Moves the system from first-come-first-served to 'first ready, first needed, first connected'. Replaces a 739 GW queue of predominantly speculative projects with a gate-based framework in which only projects that meet Readiness and Strategic Alignment criteria proceed to firm offers.
scope: Approval of CMP434 / CMP435 (CUSC) implementing the reformed Application Rules
Two-gate system: Gate 1 (indicative offers) and Gate 2 (firm offers with fixed connection dates)
Biannual application windows replacing the previous open-door
Strategic Alignment with Clean Power 2030 / SSEP / CSNP
New Progression Commitment Fee (£2,500 → £10,000 per MW)
Transitional arrangements for existing queue holders

keyParts.name: Ready Criteria
keyParts.detail: Land Route (acreage secured to Energy Density Table) or Planning Route (validated DCO / TCPA submission).

keyParts.name: Needed Criteria (Strategic Alignment)
keyParts.detail: Capacity and technology aligned to a Strategic Planning document (SSEP, CSNP, or Clean Power Plan 2030).

keyParts.name: Gate 2 offer
keyParts.detail: Firm connection date, location, costs — the 'real' offer.

keyParts.name: Gate 1 offer
keyParts.detail: Indicative; no securities; project can reapply at next biannual window.

keyParts.name: Transitional queue re-sort
keyParts.detail: Existing ~739 GW queue re-assessed against Gate 2 criteria; ~450 GW removed; queue now ~283 GW gen+storage + 99 GW demand.
